records.decision: Add a compact decision ledger with accepted-record coverage for policy, workflow, role, schema, hook, validation, and artifact changes.
records.rationale: Closeout needs machine-readable decisions for governance-affecting changes without scanning chat context.

records.decision: Require release notes coverage for behavior-changing plugin catalogs, schemas, scripts, hooks, validators, delivery gates, manifest capabilities, and reference docs.
records.rationale: Plugin behavior changes need a machine-readable operator-facing impact record before commit closeout.

records.decision: Use a topic authority map to identify canonical sources, validators, generated outputs, secondary docs, deprecated surfaces, and manifest claims.
records.rationale: Plugin claims must resolve to one active source of truth and must not treat deprecated surfaces as active authority.

records.decision: Add minimal workspace hygiene policy with bounded scan, stale check, cleanup plan, and explicit allow-path cleanup.
records.rationale: Agents need machine-readable classification for ignored runtime state, validation proof, cache state, and temporary planning state so stale files are not treated as current evidence.

records.decision: Add bears_doctor as the unified bounded JSON closeout validator for machine-first plugin closeout.
records.rationale: Closeout needs one command that checks workflow, artifacts, decisions, role routing, validation proof, runtime hygiene signals, and future-gated components without manual checklist drift.

records.decision: Use a shared @bears git hook bootstrap with thin repo-local entrypoints, effective hook proof, and hook-hot-path worker auto-start disabled.
records.rationale: Git hooks must provide bounded cheap checks and async validation queue proof without running test suites, starting services, solving issues, or printing unbounded output.

records.decision: Add commit-bound closeout metadata, exact runtime proof linkage, changelog gate linkage, and bears_doctor closeout_summary ownership.
records.rationale: Short /goal prompts need one machine-readable closeout contract instead of repeated final-report field lists in chat.

records.decision: Promote delivery_id to a required first-class closeout field with canonical first value bears-governance-kernel-v1.
records.rationale: Release-note linkage and final reporting need a stable delivery identifier that survives short /goal prompts and exact-SHA runtime proof.

records.decision: Add the first bounded self-solving issue loop that turns one eligible GitHub issue into a delivery manifest, workflow tree, sequential codex exec plan, fixer step, doctor-gated closeout, and reconciler state.
records.rationale: Short /goal prompts need issue-driven execution without one unbounded agent session or manual restatement of the architecture.

records.decision: Use labeled single-lease issue autostart for the self-solving loop.
records.rationale: Autostart must be bounded, manifest-linked, validation-gated, and closed only through issue_closeout.

records.decision: Use the canonical bears-governance-kernel-v1 identity for the enterprise issue automation release and closeout-ready delivery manifests.
records.rationale: Enterprise issue automation must not close deliveries when commit metadata, delivery manifests, doctor summaries, release records, or issue closeout output use different delivery names.

records.decision: Use issue_autostart.py as the enterprise autostart control plane with bounded tick execution, operator controls, runtime jobs/leases/events/control packets, and non-installed systemd templates.
records.rationale: Autostart must not become an unbounded daemon or launch arbitrary issue-solving work without eligibility, lease, delivery manifest, validation, and doctor gates.

records.decision: Harden sequential codex exec supervision with adapter-owned command invocation, compact prompt packets, runtime job and step packets, explicit failure classes, dirty-scope blocking, resume, status, and abort preservation.
records.rationale: Enterprise issue automation needs deterministic role-step supervision so failed, timed-out, missing-output, or dirty-scope steps cannot continue to closeout without remediation.

records.decision: Use validation_worker.py as the bounded enterprise validation lifecycle controller with one-job ticks, explicit retry limits, quarantine, service status, duplicate-running denial, and closeout-blocking remediation records.
records.rationale: Enterprise issue automation must not close issues from stale or failed validation state, and hooks must not start services or solve issues.

records.decision: Use compact local JSON observability for issue automation through reason-coded events, per-delivery audit packets, local metrics, status summaries, and reconciler summaries.
records.rationale: Operators must see why issues did or did not autostart and inspect one canonical delivery without external telemetry or raw runtime data.

records.decision: Use operator-controlled issue autostart rollout with a runtime kill-switch, ops-status packet, documented timer templates, and no service auto-installation.
records.rationale: Continuous issue automation needs explicit operator controls before timer rollout and must be stoppable without deleting evidence.

records.decision: Require canonical /srv/bears/plugins/bears checkout proof before @Bears plugin closeout.
records.rationale: The previous closeout used a hidden temporary worktree while the canonical checkout was stale, so future plugin work needs a machine closeout gate that detects redirected toplevel and core.worktree state.

records.decision: Route issue autostart work through catalog-owned repository allowlists with one write-scoped plugin route, effective hook proof, and separate read-only related-repo evidence.
records.rationale: Enterprise issue automation must not guess worktrees or mutate multiple repositories without an explicit workflow tree sequence.

records.decision: Require a read-only plugin-worktree-preflight gate before @Bears plugin edits.
records.rationale: Future @Bears plugin tasks must fail fast when cwd, git toplevel, or core.worktree does not point at /srv/bears/plugins/bears, instead of discovering the drift at closeout.

records.decision: Require automated delivery manifests and issue closeout to expose release_gate state for linked, exempt, required, and missing release-note coverage, with delivery-id references for linked entries.
records.rationale: Enterprise issue automation must not close behavior-changing deliveries without a durable release-note link or explicit issue-scoped exemption.

records.decision: Add first-class Codex exec role profiles and wire them into prompt generation.
records.rationale: Role-specific authority, forbidden actions, and output contracts prevent generic executor prompts.

records.decision: Add an executable workflow roadmap graph with catalog, schema, validator, doc, and tests.
records.rationale: Issue #413 requires roadmap-first execution so autostart can decompose large workflow work into bounded graph nodes.

records.decision: Enforce sequential codex exec output existence and explicit skill-usage recording through an executable contract instead of prompt text only.
records.rationale: The runner must fail missing files and malformed role result JSON deterministically; prompts alone cannot prove that expected outputs or skill usage records exist.

records.decision: Add executable Codex workflow decision gates.
records.rationale: Machine-readable executor decisions and allow-exec gates stop direct exec by assumption.

records.decision: Add validatable goals, principles, and principle-decision packet contracts for material workflow decisions.
records.rationale: Issue #415 requires explicit goals and principles instead of implicit chat preference.

records.decision: Restore the OpenCode agent sync entrypoint used by workspace commands and AGENTS routing.
records.rationale: The repo command surface referenced opencode_agent_sync.py, but the executable was absent; restoring it makes check and sync deterministic for repo OpenCode agents.

records.decision: Add an optional OpenCode headless executor adapter gated by explicit policy, fixed profiles, compact prompt packets, and bounded JSON result packets.
records.rationale: Issue #416 requires cheap bounded subagents without replacing @Bears closeout or autostart authority; plugin-owned sequential codex exec runtime authority is superseded by future platform microtask service constitution and research.

records.decision: Add default-deny MCP access policy for exec agents.
records.rationale: Exec agents must not inherit every MCP server or write tool.

records.decision: Add goal-state and issue-daemon orchestration before Codex exec role workflows with JSON-only decision sources, allow-exec gating, and doctor-readable live commit metadata.
records.rationale: Codex exec needs plugin-owned goal state, question-first decisions, role plans, bounded step packets, doctor/autostart integration, and closeout metadata before headless role execution or push.

records.decision: Add a validated cumulative file-context index with selector, stale detection, refresh, garbage collection, doctor checks, and goal role-plan context ids.
records.rationale: Goal orchestration needs compact source-backed file context before governed file reads so agents avoid full-file rediscovery and stale chat context.

records.decision: Add file-scoped execution planning and bounded session pool reuse as a governed metadata surface before role fanout.
records.rationale: Issue #430 requires per-file coverage, non-overlapping write units, deterministic reuse decisions, and join checks before parallel execution can run safely.

records.decision: Add workspace semantic graph, dictionary, and JSON-first metadata store policy.
records.rationale: Goal execution needs a queryable validated metadata layer with JSON in git as authority.

records.decision: Add a formal semantics kernel as the JSON authority for semantic types, directed relations, candidate facts, accepted facts, and doctor-visible status.
records.rationale: Downstream workflow logic needs validator-accepted facts and closed-world type and relation catalogs before executable question calculus, inference, graph writes, and state-machine semantics can rely on facts.

records.decision: Add executable question calculus and decision graph coverage as machine-readable gates for material workflow decisions.
records.rationale: Workflow execution needs typed deterministic questions, accepted answer proofs, semantic fact references, and decision graph gate checks before downstream execution decisions can unlock gates.

records.decision: Add deterministic Datalog-style workflow inference rules.
records.rationale: Execution permissions must derive from accepted facts and proof traces.

records.decision: Add formal workflow state-machine semantics.
records.rationale: Goal execution must advance through validated states, guards, evidence, and invariants.

records.decision: Add machine-extracted code property graph facts for governed files.
records.rationale: Semantic graph and inference need syntax-derived code facts that stale when source hashes drift.

records.decision: Add proof-driven prompt compiler and context-pack assembly.
records.rationale: Zero-context agents need deterministic prompts compiled from proofs and fresh bounded context.

records.decision: Add a sanitized read-only cross-repo infra evidence adapter that converts BearsCLOUD/bears-infra OpenCode packets into local GitOps degradation events.
records.rationale: GitOps closeout needs deterministic infra-side degradation signals without mutating bears-infra, Kubernetes, secrets, runners, or production resources.

records.decision: Accept policy invariants and validation pilots through doctor coverage reconciliation
records.rationale: Issues #457, #459, #460, #461, #462, and #463 require machine-readable validators wired into bears_doctor, test selection, artifact registry, and release notes before closeout claims are trusted.

records.decision: Add enforceable agent capability, external review, closeout reconciliation, roadmap backlog ingestion, and daemon closeout-manifest authority surfaces with schema-backed commands and tests.
records.rationale: Agent workflow quality and issue closeout cannot rely on chat, docs-only claims, or runtime-only manifests; the plugin needs repo-visible validation commands, ledgers, reports, fixture-backed acceptance tests, and commit-bound closeout authority before GitHub issue closure.

records.decision: Add bounded old backlog reconciliation and proof-based classification.
records.rationale: Issue #411 requires old open issues to be closed only with exact proof and otherwise left classified.

records.decision: Add governed repository onboarding inventory and read-only default routing.
records.rationale: Issue #412 requires non-plugin repositories to be represented without unsafe write permission and write scope to require explicit proof.

records.decision: Add context surface budget and split-decision governance.
records.rationale: Issue #418 requires bounded skill and role context surfaces with oversized and mixed-authority split gates.

records.decision: Add compact per-commit agent usage and context ledger.
records.rationale: Issue #419 requires machine-readable usage events, commit ledgers, regression diffs, and doctor status without raw content.

records.decision: Add role degradation detection and remediation plans.
records.rationale: Issue #420 requires degradation events, manual review blocking, audit actions, and doctor status.

records.decision: Add machine-readable GitOps state, degradation, and rollback gates for delivery closeout.
records.rationale: Issue #421 requires delivery state transitions, degradation events, rollback policy, and closeout blocking evidence.

records.decision: Add bounded orchestrator invariant and model-check result gates.
records.rationale: Issue #443 requires explicit safety, liveness, scope, cost, parallelism, closeout, storage authority, and human review invariants.

records.decision: Add process-mining feedback loop and deduped improvement candidates.
records.rationale: Issue #446 requires workflow trace comparison, non-fitting traces, validator gaps, context-budget candidates, and dry-run issue candidates.

records.decision: Add the tgsearch-live-gate Kubernetes Git/CD selector and Job completion wait support.
records.rationale: TG One Chat Intelligence live PASS requires Kubernetes desired state, local CD, digest-pinned image evidence, and Job completion evidence instead of local deploy paths.

records.decision: Accept canonical /srv/bears/dev/platform route ownership plus exact Kubernetes data-service, daemon, ledger, and archive-manifest route registration without widening runtime, deployment, secret, or product-source authority.
records.rationale: The role gate must prevent legacy platform path drift and broad parent fallback while allowing only exact service lanes and route-registration metadata to proceed through local commit validation.

records.decision: Add gate-bound Clarification Architect role contracts and schemas.
records.rationale: Issue #442 requires deterministic clarification packets before planning or implementation so missing goal facts are explicit, typed, and closeable.

records.decision: Self-heal is runtime repair work and must not be modeled as a GitHub issue, issue intake packet, issue-autostart job, issue comment, issue closeout, or active issue-autostart lease wait.
records.rationale: Runtime failures need the same daemon dispatch, Codex Exec, validation, commit, and doctor gates while preserving other agents dirty paths through owned-path staging only; live self-heal approval is issue-free and does not use issue-autostart approval helpers.

records.decision: Route Telegram product-app migration catalogs through the canonical BearsCLOUD/apps product-app repository and treat old The Ants catalog references as migration/archive evidence.
records.rationale: The apps monorepo policy requires old product app repositories and catalog issue routing to link to BearsCLOUD/apps instead of preserving separate canonical app repositories.

records.decision: Accept apps consolidation governance updates across plugin role profiles, local_cd policy, subagent orchestration policy, and Codex Telegram operator feedback workflow.
records.rationale: The apps monorepo consolidation requires plugin governance to prevent nested apps paths, route product-app source into BearsCLOUD/apps, keep runtime deployment under Kubernetes desired state and local_cd, and use skill-driven codex-telegram MCP operator feedback instead of a PreToolUse hook.

records.decision: Add GitHub Project and Issue driven subagent governance with parent-control-only App Dev, Subagents role-lane rules, and a GitHub Projects/Issues orchestrator role.
records.rationale: The operator goal requires GitHub Projects, Issues, PR metadata, Actions metadata, Releases metadata, and @Bears L2/L3 subagent roles to be first-class workflow surfaces while preventing parent and L2 implementation authority.

records.decision: Add a machine-readable GitHub Project subagents catalog and validator, and restore execution-kernel authority-map support files required by local commit validation.
records.rationale: The app-dev workflow needs deterministic coverage for GitHub Projects, Issues, sub-issues, PRs, Actions, Releases, Discussions, Wiki, Packages, Deployments, security metadata, and repository collaboration metadata, while validation drift must not block exact-SHA closeout.

records.decision: Restore issue_state_reconciler as an execution-kernel authority support script.
records.rationale: bears_doctor imports issue_state_reconciler and delivery manifests require solved-open issue reconciliation for local commit validation.

records.decision: Restore catalog-listed doctor support scripts as minimal metadata validators.
records.rationale: bears_doctor.validate_all requires every catalog-listed execution support script to exist and expose bounded JSON/status surfaces for local commit validation.

records.decision: Register GitHub Project subagent and execution-kernel support files in test-selection and Pants graph routing.
records.rationale: Local commit validation uses the test-selection catalog and Pants graph catalog; newly restored governance docs and scripts must not remain unmatched paths.

records.decision: Route every active plugin skill SKILL.md through exact platform role coverage and bind Infisical ops to Kubernetes secret-reference governance.
records.rationale: Parent-only skill routing caused drift for Infisical and Python codeflow work; exact route coverage plus compact routers prevents local control-plane and Kubernetes runtime proof from being mixed.

records.decision: Project mandate uses a small registry-gated mandate-packet for one target and must not build a workspace artifact inventory.
records.rationale: Agents were getting stuck because the skill asked for broad checks; the packet now gives exact read_paths and forbids workspace scans.

records.decision: PreToolUse blocks local test execution and Kubernetes history archaeology while allowing current CI evidence lookup.
records.rationale: Operator required tests to be CI-owned and Kubernetes history reads to stay bounded; hook enforcement keeps agent context small and validation evidence current.

records.decision: Enforce canonical app/project terms in plugin governance policy and repo/target wording.
records.rationale: Plugin governance must distinguish product app source from plugin/runtime surfaces and GitHub Project planning boards from filesystem/source ownership.

records.decision: Route Bears acceptance to platform Dagger ObjectiveRuntimeProof or final Kubernetes live proof instead of a separate test, contract, or validation PASS layer.
records.rationale: Agents need one objective runtime proof surface so static checks remain safety signals and cannot replace runtime evidence.

contradictions: Superseded by main-push automatic diagnostics policy on 2026-07-04.
records.decision: Superseded trigger model for plugin validate workflow and archived GitHub dev-CD policy checker.
records.rationale: Superseded because active CI policy now requires fast automatic diagnostics on main push while keeping emergency full suite operator-dispatched.

records.decision: Retire deleted Telegram disabled-skill catalog and helper surfaces from active plugin governance.
records.rationale: Disabled Telegram skill dirs were removed from skills/, so coupled catalog and helper files must not stay as active authoritative surfaces.

records.decision: Keep app workflow CI boundaries aligned with ci-requirements and sequential exec surfaces.
records.rationale: Staged workflow-policy changes need one accepted ledger entry covering the new CI boundary paths.

records.decision: Remove the obsolete codex workflow policy script after its references were retired.
records.rationale: The deleted script is no longer an active authority surface and needs a machine-readable accepted record for closeout.

records.decision: Keep the development workflow packet validator self-contained after development-workflow-orchestration and Speckit overlay skills were consolidated into app workflow skills.
records.rationale: The active plugin must not depend on removed skill contract files or missing test files, and CI safety catalogs must reflect the app-* skill boundary.

records.decision: Allow the plugin validate workflow to run fast automatic diagnostics on main push while keeping emergency full suite behind operator workflow_dispatch.
records.rationale: The operator policy requires quick automatic CI after commit; the archived GitHub dev-CD checker must enforce main-only push diagnostics rather than workflow_dispatch-only diagnostics.

records.decision: Retire the standalone legacy constitution layer and make subagents-roles the executable role governance owner with route, audit, and ledger commands.
records.rationale: The operator-approved migration removes duplicate legacy constitution authority, keeps repeatable role governance in executable @Bears surfaces, and records principle coverage in a tracked ledger.

records.decision: Add app functional graph and app task ledger helper surfaces for app-plan and app-dev.
records.rationale: App execution tasks need machine-readable functionality refs, graph node refs, async/API/state refs, and notification-only Issue handling before L2/L3 dispatch.

records.decision: Register app functional graph orchestrator in the agent sandbox matrix.
records.rationale: The new helper role needs deterministic sandbox validation coverage in validate_overlay.

records.decision: Use bears-plugin as the canonical @Bears marketplace key for plugin cache sync and installation.
records.rationale: The previous marketplace key pointed installed cache and upgrade attempts at stale marketplace naming while the canonical plugin repository is BearsCLOUD/bears_plugin.

records.decision: Add autoCI graph zone selection to app task ledgers and local commit validation; agents record computed zones and expected status names while autoCI owns execution.
records.rationale: The operator cleanup plan moves manual route, test, and validator decisions into automatic CI/local commit validation and requires app ledgers to carry machine-readable status expectations.

records.decision: Install/update uses generated local path config outside plugin source and LCV blocks newly introduced server-specific absolute paths.
records.rationale: Operator required portable plugin source, root-owned commit wording, and pre-push autoCI/LCV error checks without manual validator execution during development.

records.decision: Require instruction-hardening codex exec rows to start with prompt-file plus role-file context only through an isolated exec runner with nonessential feature surfaces disabled.
records.rationale: Exec matrix rows were invalidly expensive when inherited config, plugin context, skills, MCP/app context, multi-agent tools, or auto-loaded instruction files entered startup or tool context.

records.decision: Host instruction zones export in the @Bears plugin and expose only the mcp server zones tool.
records.rationale: Agents need one plugin-native normalized instruction graph source while legacy script paths stay compatible.

records.decision: Require the instruction-hardening MCP fallback helper to use a read-only stdio client that emits bounded JSON evidence and never imports scanner internals for startup or graph retrieval.

live_confirmation.summary: The instruction-artifacts MCP reference is part of the scanned graph and documents the bounded hardening MCP evidence surface.

records.rationale: The approved micro-task needs a portable evidence helper for MCP correctness so instruction hardening can inspect bounded startup packets without claiming scanner output as operator authority.

records.decision: Harden small instruction-policy catalog wording while preserving each catalog rule's existing authority and scope.
records.rationale: The instruction-hardening goal requires current mutable policy prose to avoid weak action words without rewriting historical evidence or changing machine-owned policy semantics.

records.decision: Harden counted platform role catalog policy wording while preserving role routing authority and safety boundaries.
records.rationale: The instruction-hardening goal requires mutable platform role policy prose to avoid weak action words without changing role ownership, deploy/runtime/secret boundaries, branch-protection exact required check list wording, or status-gate semantics.

records.decision: Catalog weak-term scoring must read only selected human-policy string fields and must not fall back to scanning full JSON machine metadata when no selected field exists.
records.rationale: The instruction-hardening goal requires the MCP/helper queue to identify mutable instruction prose, not commands, identifiers, required-status-check terms, or catalog parity phrases that would cause semantic drift if rewritten.

records.decision: Harden role TOML selection and validation-boundary prose without changing role routing, sandbox settings, model settings, forbidden actions, or evidence requirements.
records.rationale: The instruction-hardening goal requires role-profile prose to avoid weak action words while preserving role ownership, helper or reviewer status, validation boundaries, and CI-owned safety status handling.
